name: Pah Tum
author: AGP
description: Pah Tum (Pa Tum), an ancient Near-Eastern / Assyrian grid game described in R. C. Bell's 'Board and Table Games'. On a 7x7 grid a fixed, symmetric set of blocked cells holds no stone; players alternately place one stone of their colour on any empty, non-blocked cell until every playable cell is filled. Each maximal horizontal or vertical run of 3+ same-colour stones scores by an escalating table (3->3, 4->10, 5->25, 6->56, 7->88); diagonals never score. Highest total wins; equal totals draw.
